title: senecio laceratus
intro: Senecio laceratus, also known as Jacob's ladder or green dew of the field, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive, fern-like foliage and tall spikes of small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Senecio laceratus include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting the immune system. Senecio laceratus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although proper dosing and caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
